creator: glarkin@sourcehosting.net
text:
Hi Carlo,

It looks like your update to bzr-git depends on the fix for
bzr-builder.  I have discovered that bzr-builder is generating the error
message about debian_bundle.  There are a lot of Debian-specific bits in
the bzr-builder code.  bzr-git imports bzr-builder, which then throws
the import exception.

I "fixed" the problem by simply commenting out the import code in
bzr-builder like so:

--- cmds.py.orig        2011-08-23 13:35:18.000000000 -0400
+++ cmds.py     2011-08-23 13:30:57.000000000 -0400
@@ -29,12 +29,12 @@
 import subprocess
 import tempfile

-try:
-    from debian import changelog, deb822
-except ImportError:
-    # In older versions of python-debian the main package was named
-    # debian_bundle
-    from debian_bundle import changelog, deb822
+# try:
+#     from debian import changelog, deb822
+# except ImportError:
+#     # In older versions of python-debian the main package was named
+#     # debian_bundle
+#     from debian_bundle import changelog, deb822

 from bzrlib import (
         errors,
--- recipe.py.orig      2011-08-23 13:35:26.000000000 -0400
+++ recipe.py   2011-08-23 13:31:09.000000000 -0400
@@ -39,10 +39,10 @@
     NoWhoami = object()


-try:
-    from debian import changelog
-except ImportError:
-    from debian_bundle import changelog
+# try:
+#     from debian import changelog
+# except ImportError:
+#     from debian_bundle import changelog

 try:
     MergeIntoMerger = merge.MergeIntoMerger

Is that sufficient?  If so, I'll commit that change, close this PR and
then commit the update to bzr-git.

Thank you,
Greg



	<http://www.linkedin.com/e/sig/146767/>
